The Fedora Project is known for, among other things, having a well-defined set of processes for just about everything. It has extensive packaging guidelines that deal with the complexities of creating RPMs to install software, as well as processes for managing the legal questions that arise around shipping software. Fedora also has a well-defined change process for dealing with self-contained technical changes as well as major changes to the distribution, and other issues as they arise. At the moment, though, the project seems to be experiencing a sort of midlife crisis as it re-examines several of its change processes at once to determine if they are still effective.

Evolution

As a vendor-sponsored project, Fedora has always had to walk a difficult path in serving its users, contributor community, and corporate masters; what makes one party the happiest may be a source of angst for another. Red Hat's desire to trial technologies in Fedora does not always spark joy among contributors and users. Volunteer contributors may want to push ideas that are of little interest to Red Hat (at least at the time), or may even conflict with its choices. For example, Fedora's choice of Btrfs as the default filesystem contrasts with Red Hat's decision not to support it in Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL).

Users want easy access to problematic software, such as patent-encumbered games or codecs, but shipping those could create expensive legal headaches for Red Hat. Attempts to make Fedora more user-friendly, say by setting the default for the $EDITOR environment variable to GNU nano, may not please some developers.

Fedora had little in the way of governance or policy when the project launched in 2003 as a kind of replacement for Red Hat Linux. Early on, Red Hat entertained the idea of creating a "Fedora Foundation" that would give the project some independence, and then pulled back from that in 2006. Some of the mechanisms that were put in place in anticipation of the foundation, such as a Fedora Board and Fedora Extras Steering Committee, evolved into the Fedora Council and Fedora Engineering Steering Committee (FESCo) over time.

There have been a number of times when there was friction between corporate and community priorities; but the project has more or less made it work over the years. It has done this by discussing the problems, finding some kind of consensus, documenting the policies that are hammered out along the way, and then using them for new decisions. See, for example, Tom Callaway's overview of how Fedora's legal policies were developed. Over the years, Fedora's governance and policies have become something of a model for other open-source projects.

The project's governance has had one constant: Red Hat has the final say. As former Fedora Project Leader (FPL) Max Spevack noted when Red Hat dumped the idea of an independent foundation:

Red Hat *must* maintain a certain amount of control over Fedora decisions, because Red Hat's business model *depends* upon Fedora. Red Hat contributes millions of dollars in staff and resources to the success of Fedora, and Red Hat also accepts all of the legal risk for Fedora. Therefore, Red Hat will sometimes need to make tough decisions about Fedora. We won't do it often, and when we do, we will discuss the rationale behind such decisions as openly as we can.

Just because Red Hat has power over Fedora does not mean that the company wants to use it, he said. Nor did it want to make all the important decisions about Fedora: effective community-driven decision making would be a direct measure of Fedora's success. "We aim to set the standard for open source innovation. A truly open Fedora Project is what makes that possible."

Sandbox

In the past year or so, though, the project's processes that have worked until now seem to be coming into question more and more often: usually when they seem to butt up against Red Hat's priorities. For example, last year's deliberation on Fedora's AI-assisted contribution policy that showed a disconnect between Red Hat's desire to experiment with AI in Fedora and contributors who wanted Fedora's policy to be much less friendly to AI.

In March, current FPL Jef Spaleta proposed a technology-innovation-lifecycle process he dubbed the "Fedora Sandbox" for "experimental features, components, output, process, or services".

The driver for Spaleta's sandbox idea seemed to be related to frustrations that Red Hat leadership had with pushing its experiments into Fedora; the project's existing processes, which involve compliance with Fedora's strict packaging and license policies as well as a need to gain community buy-in, could slow or stymie acceptance of things Red Hat was going to do for RHEL anyway.

Having to maintain those projects outside of Fedora, which ultimately serves as the foundation for a RHEL release, is a likely source of frustration for folks working on both—not to mention their managers and Red Hat leadership who (not unreasonably) just want features to land in RHEL on schedule to make customers happy.

Spaleta's sandbox process would have allowed experiments to be conducted in Fedora even if they broke "non legally binding" policies as long as they had a "reasonable path forward towards resolution" before being fully integrated into the project or distribution. He proposed that the sandbox would be in addition to the existing change processes and community initiatives that can be used to set longer-term goals for Fedora, such as the completed initiative to create the Fedora IoT project, or the current Git Forge initiative to replace the Pagure collaboration platform with the Forgejo-based Fedora Forge service. The sandbox proposal has not been accepted (or rejected) yet.

Initiative friction

Red Hat developer Gordon Messmer proposed an AI developer desktop initiative, on March 31, with an aim to "build a thriving community around AI technologies" within Fedora. That proposal met with some opposition from the community, with objections ranging from a dislike of AI to complaints about potentially changing Fedora's policy for out-of-tree kernel modules. Ultimately the proposal was discussed by the Fedora Council, initially approved, and then blocked at the last minute when council member Justin Wheeler changed his vote on May 8. Council member Miro Hrončok, likewise, also changed his vote on May 13 saying that "the Fedora community is not supportive of this initiative as is".

Another Red Hat idea, an automated approach to building an operating system called Project Hummingbird, was raised on the Fedora development list in April. It was greeted with some interest, as well as some confusion about how it might differ from other Fedora variants, but it didn't seem to face much opposition. It was never formally proposed, though McCarty said he planned to do so through Spaleta's sandbox initiative.

Instead, Red Hat bypassed public processes entirely. It asked the council in private for approval to use the Fedora trademark so that it could announce the project at Red Hat Summit on May 12. Fedora contributors outside of Red Hat were surprised and confused by the announcement. Michael Gruber, for example, wondered whether Hummingbird was legitimately a Fedora project or not. "There might be even some good ideas in there, but given how this started and how it is communicated I can put zero trust in this."

Red Hat employee and Fedora contributor Adam Williamson said it would have been ideal for Red Hat to make the request more openly, but he was happy that the company was trying to do the Hummingbird project within Fedora rather than doing an end run around the project. The more Red Hat has to do outside Fedora, he reasoned, the greater the risk that the company will question its funding of the project.

From inside Red Hat, especially if you don't work on Fedora, it is possible/tempting to look at Fedora as a source of strife. It's got all these people in it, with opinions, who aren't on the payroll! You can't tell them what to do or think or complain to their manager! They have eternal arguments about everything! You have to write a wiki page and convince some person you've never heard of that your idea is good! Who needs this?

So we're kind of constantly fighting a tendency for RH to just spin stuff up in channels it 100% controls, which tends to seem easiest at first then turn out to be a mess after a few years.

Christopher Klooz, however, worried that the funding justification meant that Fedora could be "step by step transformed into a corporate unit of RH". He added that it was already unclear "when the Council acts as agent of RH and when as agent of the community".

Williamson replied that he understood the point, but said that this was a "an ever-present tension" that Fedora has had to manage nearly as long as it's been around:

The way Fedora can "compete" is not by turning into the thing some parts of RH might superficially think they want - an upstream project that's open source in license terms but closely-controlled in governance - but by being the thing they need - a loosely-coupled upstream project with passionate and involved people who will make things awkward and uncomfortable at times but usually produce a better end result over the long term, and act as a useful check on RH's perceptions at times.

Red Hat employees who care about Fedora, he said, have to keep selling that vision and making sure that it works. The Hummingbird discussion petered out not long after Williamson's reply, but it appears that Fedora's decision-making bodies have been mulling over how things are done.

Hit pause

On July 1, Aoife Moloney, Fedora's "change wrangler", announced that the Fedora Council was proposing a pause to Fedora's community initiatives process. Existing initiatives would continue as planned, she said, though "the administrative framework around them may evolve".

The AI developer desktop, she said, had shown that the initiatives process had failed as a framework "where new ideas can surface, receive respectful feedback, and gain Council support for work that fits the project's present and/or future". The nature of the failure, however, was unspecified. One can imagine that the various participants in that discussion might well agree that something had not gone well, but what that something was would depend entirely on the observer's point of view.

The council, Moloney said, wanted to work out a new method of setting strategic direction "in an open, transparent way that more intentionally includes the community voice." The council recognized that it needed to be "better at being more open in our discussions and decision making" because much of the work that leads to proposals "happens under the radar before official approval processes kick in". At the moment the existing "approval pipeline" for initiatives involves the council performing trademark review, and then FESCo reviewing change proposals. That works well, she said, but misses "early and inclusive discussion for everyone across the project". Therefore, the council would be looking at the sandbox proposal closely as an alternative to initiatives or as a complement to some other process that it might develop.

The announcement phrased the pause as a proposal, but Moloney also closed the discussion on the AI developer desktop proposal saying that the council was now unable to consider it "as a result of halting the Community Initiatives process." The council would return to the question of "whether Community initiatives should be retired or revamped once this discussion has reached some kind of conclusion".

Changes to changes

Shortly before the council had made its announcement, FESCo member "Maxwell G" started a discussion on the Fedora development mailing list to gather feedback on how the changes process could be improved. He said he was not proposing anything specific, but wanted to throw out some ideas and see what other people thought.

For example, he wondered if it was time to move away from using Fedora's wiki for proposals, as well as the Wikitext formatting that goes along with it. The formatting often escapes the wiki and makes its way to other fora; for example, the Btrfs change proposal for Fedora 33 from 2020 has a combination of plain text, MediaWiki formatting, and HTML <span> tags that makes it unpleasant to attempt to decipher in any mail client.

He suggested that changes should be in Markdown or "some format that can easily be converted", and stored as text files in a Git repository rather than on the wiki. The change owners could file proposals as a merge request, which would be reviewed and merged by the change wrangler. He also floated the idea of moving away from the Discourse forum as the primary source of truth for change proposals; he also wanted to stop the practice of having discussions on changes take place both on the forum and Fedora's development list:

Cross-posting to Discourse was proposed as an experiment in fesco [ticket] #2989, but there was never a decision made about whether to stop or continue with the experiment. I find the fractured discussions between devel@ and Discourse hard to follow. I think the Discourse setup makes it easier for discussions to "accelerate" or become toxic or repetitive. I don't think it's any better at handling large threads.

The suggestion to consolidate discussions on the mailing list seemed popular. Michal Schorm agreed that splitting discussions between the mailing list and forum was bad, especially for the change owners who had to follow both. Björn Persson also agreed that the discussions needed to be consolidated, but he observed that it was much worse than just following a forum and mailing list discussion. "When I went through the Change process, the state of the Change was fractured over the wiki, Pagure, Bugzilla and even Gitlab." He counted ten different discussion fora and issue trackers, all in their own silos. "At least Pagure and Bugzilla are good at sending me email when someone writes a comment."

Kevin Fenzi, however, thought that the practice of using the forum for change requests should continue. He acknowledged that there were some problems with that, but it meant Fedora received feedback "from people who are not otherwise involved and sometimes [it is] very useful". He felt that it was important to hear from new voices and to accept feedback "in the place where they are".

Former Fedora program manager Ben Cotton, who had worked closely with the change process during his tenure, thought that the process should be modernized, but suggested that a better approach would be to think about what the process should look like at a high level, then think about how to implement it.

There was a suggestion from Martin Kolman that Fedora should build a simple web application to manage the change process. It would be less daunting for new contributors, he said, and could be designed to validate information before a change was submitted. The idea of another homegrown application to maintain, however, did not win supporters. Daniel P. Berrangé said that Fedora had "a long (and disappointing) track record" of building things and then being unable to support them in the long run. "I can't see it being a good use of resources to build & maintain a custom app for this."

Moloney, who works with changes as part of her job, weighed in on June 30. She said she had agreed with some of the suggestions, such as restricting change discussions to the mailing list, but asked that participants in the discussion to ask themselves how much of hands-on involvement they had with the mechanics of the process "before suggesting ways to engineer a brand new way to do it". She also noted that there were many moving parts already, such as the move to Fedora Forge, and thought it might be a good idea to let the dust settle from those "and then see what we're missing".

Red Hat engineering manager Brendan Conoboy also spoke up with his view of Fedora's processes from the outside looking in. Overall, he thought that the change processes work pretty well for the Linux distribution. "Here's what doesn't work: changes that fit poorly inside existing processes." He cited the recent two-factor authentication discussion and the AI developer desktop proposal as examples. "The bar to clear to initiate change in a way every interest feels respected is tough to such a degree that even initiating a conversation is fraught."

Maxwell G pointed out that the AI discussion "hit a lot of pain points", some of which were procedural (such as the council failing to clearly announce its intent to vote on the proposal), but there were also social and political disagreements as well. In any case, he said, that was an initiative rather than a change proposal—and that process was under separate review by the council. Conoboy replied, "we knew the [AI] subject matter was controversial, and it seemed that following a documented process would provide a better framework for constructive feedback". Perhaps it did, he said, but it might have gone better if the idea had been socialized with FESCo first.

Outcome

After the discussion had wound down Maxwell G replied on July 7, with his summary of the conversation. His first takeaway was that he should have had a more detailed problem statement that had more context about what he was looking for from the discussion. He found that there was some support for a Git-based workflow, but the wiki-based workflow also had many supporters; many ideas were put forth, but no clear winners.

What was clear, however, was that the split between the mailing list and forum "creates a frustrating burden for Change Owners and other people trying to follow the discussion". The project had tried the "split-brain approach for development discussion" for three years, and it was not working. He said he would focus on solving that pain point first, and then would come back with ideas for a different wiki-based process at a later date.

Given that it is vacation season in much of the world, there is a good chance that the various efforts to modify Fedora's processes will see little movement in the next month or two. It seems likely, though, that there will be some shakeups to the project's processes once Fedora contributors return from holiday and pick up where these conversations have left off.

He said he would focus on solving that pain point first, and then would come back with ideas for a different wiki-based process at a later date. Given that it is vacation season in much of the world, there is a good chance that the various efforts to modify Fedora's processes will see little movement in the next month or two. It seems likely, though, that there will be some shakeups to the project's processes once Fedora contributors return from holiday and pick up where these conversations have left off. blocks.html_en: The Fedora Project is known for, among other things, having a well-defined set of processes for just about everything. It has extensive packaging guidelines that deal with the complexities of creating RPMs to install software, as well as processes for managing the legal questions that arise around shipping software. Fedora also has a well-defined change process for dealing with self-contained technical changes as well as major changes to the distribution, and other issues as they arise. At the moment, though, the project seems to be experiencing a sort of midlife crisis as it re-examines several of its change processes at once to determine if they are still effective.

sentences.en: The Fedora Project is known for, among other things, having a well-defined set of processes for just about everything.
sentences.zh: Fedora 项目因拥有一套适用于几乎所有事物的明确定义的流程而闻名。

sentences.en: It has extensive packaging guidelines that deal with the complexities of creating RPMs to install software, as well as processes for managing the legal questions that arise around shipping software.
sentences.zh: 它具有广泛的打包指南，可处理创建 RPM 来安装软件的复杂性，以及管理与运输软件相关的法律问题的流程。

sentences.en: Fedora also has a well-defined change process for dealing with self-contained technical changes as well as major changes to the distribution, and other issues as they arise.
sentences.zh: Fedora 还有一个定义明确的变更流程，用于处理独立的技术变更以及发行版的重大变更以及出现的其他问题。

sentences.en: At the moment, though, the project seems to be experiencing a sort of midlife crisis as it re-examines several of its change processes at once to determine if they are still effective.
sentences.zh: 但目前，该项目似乎正在经历某种中年危机，因为它同时重新检查了几个变革流程，以确定它们是否仍然有效。

blocks.html_en: Evolution

sentences.en: Evolution
sentences.zh: 进化

blocks.html_en: As a vendor-sponsored project, Fedora has always had to walk a difficult path in serving its users, contributor community, and corporate masters; what makes one party the happiest may be a source of angst for another. Red Hat's desire to trial technologies in Fedora does not always spark joy among contributors and users. Volunteer contributors may want to push ideas that are of little interest to Red Hat (at least at the time), or may even conflict with its choices. For example, Fedora's choice of Btrfs as the default filesystem contrasts with Red Hat's decision not to support it in Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL).

sentences.en: As a vendor-sponsored project, Fedora has always had to walk a difficult path in serving its users, contributor community, and corporate masters; what makes one party the happiest may be a source of angst for another.
sentences.zh: 作为一个由厂商赞助的项目，Fedora 在服务用户、贡献者社区和企业大师方面始终要走一条艰难的道路；让一方最快乐的事情可能会成为另一方焦虑的根源。

sentences.en: Red Hat's desire to trial technologies in Fedora does not always spark joy among contributors and users.
sentences.zh: 红帽想要在 Fedora 中试用技术的愿望并不总能让贡献者和用户感到高兴。

sentences.en: Volunteer contributors may want to push ideas that are of little interest to Red Hat (at least at the time), or may even conflict with its choices.
sentences.zh: 志愿者贡献者可能想要推动红帽不感兴趣的想法（至少在当时），甚至可能与其选择相冲突。

sentences.en: For example, Fedora's choice of Btrfs as the default filesystem contrasts with Red Hat's decision not to support it in Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL).
sentences.zh: 例如，Fedora 选择 Btrfs 作为默认文件系统，而 Red Hat 决定在 Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) 中不支持它。

blocks.html_en: Users want easy access to problematic software, such as patent-encumbered games or codecs, but shipping those could create expensive legal headaches for Red Hat. Attempts to make Fedora more user-friendly, say by setting the default for the $EDITOR environment variable to GNU nano, may not please some developers.

sentences.en: Users want easy access to problematic software, such as patent-encumbered games or codecs, but shipping those could create expensive legal headaches for Red Hat.
sentences.zh: 用户希望轻松访问有问题的软件，例如受专利保护的游戏或编解码器，但运送这些软件可能会给红帽带来昂贵的法律麻烦。

sentences.en: Attempts to make Fedora more user-friendly, say by setting the default for the $EDITOR environment variable to GNU nano, may not please some developers.
sentences.zh: 尝试使 Fedora 更加用户友好，例如将 $EDITOR 环境变量的默认值设置为 GNU nano，可能不会令某些开发人员满意。

blocks.html_en: Fedora had little in the way of governance or policy when the project launched in 2003 as a kind of replacement for Red Hat Linux. Early on, Red Hat entertained the idea of creating a "Fedora Foundation" that would give the project some independence, and then pulled back from that in 2006. Some of the mechanisms that were put in place in anticipation of the foundation, such as a Fedora Board and Fedora Extras Steering Committee, evolved into the Fedora Council and Fedora Engineering Steering Committee (FESCo) over time.

sentences.en: Fedora had little in the way of governance or policy when the project launched in 2003 as a kind of replacement for Red Hat Linux.
sentences.zh: 当 Fedora 项目于 2003 年作为 Red Hat Linux 的替代品启动时，它几乎没有任何治理或政策。

sentences.en: Early on, Red Hat entertained the idea of creating a "Fedora Foundation" that would give the project some independence, and then pulled back from that in 2006.
sentences.zh: 早期，红帽曾考虑过创建一个“Fedora 基金会”来赋予该项目一定的独立性，但随后在 2006 年撤回了这一想法。

sentences.en: Some of the mechanisms that were put in place in anticipation of the foundation, such as a Fedora Board and Fedora Extras Steering Committee, evolved into the Fedora Council and Fedora Engineering Steering Committee (FESCo) over time.
sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，一些在基金会成立时建立的机制，例如 Fedora 董事会和 Fedora Extras 指导委员会，逐渐演变为 Fedora 理事会和 Fedora 工程指导委员会 (FESCo)。

blocks.html_en: There have been a number of times when there was friction between corporate and community priorities; but the project has more or less made it work over the years. It has done this by discussing the problems, finding some kind of consensus, documenting the policies that are hammered out along the way, and then using them for new decisions. See, for example, Tom Callaway's overview of how Fedora's legal policies were developed. Over the years, Fedora's governance and policies have become something of a model for other open-source projects.

sentences.en: There have been a number of times when there was friction between corporate and community priorities; but the project has more or less made it work over the years.
sentences.zh: 企业和社区的优先事项之间曾多次发生过摩擦；但这些年来该项目或多或少已经发挥了作用。

sentences.en: It has done this by discussing the problems, finding some kind of consensus, documenting the policies that are hammered out along the way, and then using them for new decisions.
sentences.zh: 它通过讨论问题、找到某种共识、记录一路上敲定的政策，然后将其用于新的决策来做到这一点。

sentences.en: See, for example, Tom Callaway's overview of how Fedora's legal policies were developed.
sentences.zh: 例如，请参阅 Tom Callaway 对 Fedora 法律政策如何制定的概述。

sentences.en: Over the years, Fedora's governance and policies have become something of a model for other open-source projects.
sentences.zh: 多年来，Fedora 的治理和政策已经成为其他开源项目的典范。

blocks.html_en: The project's governance has had one constant: Red Hat has the final say. As former Fedora Project Leader (FPL) Max Spevack noted when Red Hat dumped the idea of an independent foundation:

sentences.en: The project's governance has had one constant: Red Hat has the final say.
sentences.zh: 该项目的治理有一个不变的事实：红帽拥有最终决定权。

sentences.en: As former Fedora Project Leader (FPL) Max Spevack noted when Red Hat dumped the idea of an independent foundation:
sentences.zh: 正如前 Fedora 项目负责人 (FPL) Max Spevack 在红帽放弃独立基金会的想法时指出的那样：

blocks.html_en: Red Hat *must* maintain a certain amount of control over Fedora decisions, because Red Hat's business model *depends* upon Fedora. Red Hat contributes millions of dollars in staff and resources to the success of Fedora, and Red Hat also accepts all of the legal risk for Fedora. Therefore, Red Hat will sometimes need to make tough decisions about Fedora. We won't do it often, and when we do, we will discuss the rationale behind such decisions as openly as we can.

sentences.en: Red Hat *must* maintain a certain amount of control over Fedora decisions, because Red Hat's business model *depends* upon Fedora.
sentences.zh: 红帽“必须”对 Fedora 决策保持一定程度的控制，因为红帽的商业模式“依赖于”Fedora。

sentences.en: Red Hat contributes millions of dollars in staff and resources to the success of Fedora, and Red Hat also accepts all of the legal risk for Fedora.
sentences.zh: 红帽为 Fedora 的成功贡献了数百万美元的员工和资源，红帽也承担了 Fedora 的所有法律风险。

sentences.en: Therefore, Red Hat will sometimes need to make tough decisions about Fedora.
sentences.zh: 因此，Red Hat 有时需要对 Fedora 做出艰难的决定。

sentences.en: We won't do it often, and when we do, we will discuss the rationale behind such decisions as openly as we can.
sentences.zh: 我们不会经常这样做，当我们这样做时，我们将尽可能公开地讨论此类决定背后的理由。

blocks.html_en: Red Hat *must* maintain a certain amount of control over Fedora decisions, because Red Hat's business model *depends* upon Fedora. Red Hat contributes millions of dollars in staff and resources to the success of Fedora, and Red Hat also accepts all of the legal risk for Fedora. Therefore, Red Hat will sometimes need to make tough decisions about Fedora. We won't do it often, and when we do, we will discuss the rationale behind such decisions as openly as we can.

sentences.en: Red Hat *must* maintain a certain amount of control over Fedora decisions, because Red Hat's business model *depends* upon Fedora.
sentences.zh: 红帽“必须”对 Fedora 决策保持一定程度的控制，因为红帽的商业模式“依赖于”Fedora。

sentences.en: Red Hat contributes millions of dollars in staff and resources to the success of Fedora, and Red Hat also accepts all of the legal risk for Fedora.
sentences.zh: 红帽为 Fedora 的成功贡献了数百万美元的员工和资源，红帽也承担了 Fedora 的所有法律风险。

sentences.en: Therefore, Red Hat will sometimes need to make tough decisions about Fedora.
sentences.zh: 因此，Red Hat 有时需要对 Fedora 做出艰难的决定。

sentences.en: We won't do it often, and when we do, we will discuss the rationale behind such decisions as openly as we can.
sentences.zh: 我们不会经常这样做，当我们这样做时，我们将尽可能公开地讨论此类决定背后的理由。

blocks.html_en: Just because Red Hat has power over Fedora does not mean that the company wants to use it, he said. Nor did it want to make all the important decisions about Fedora: effective community-driven decision making would be a direct measure of Fedora's success. "We aim to set the standard for open source innovation. A truly open Fedora Project is what makes that possible."

sentences.en: Just because Red Hat has power over Fedora does not mean that the company wants to use it, he said.
sentences.zh: 他说，仅仅因为红帽拥有对 Fedora 的控制权，并不意味着该公司想要使用它。

sentences.en: Nor did it want to make all the important decisions about Fedora: effective community-driven decision making would be a direct measure of Fedora's success.
sentences.zh: 它也不想做出有关 Fedora 的所有重要决策：有效的社区驱动决策将是 Fedora 成功的直接衡量标准。

sentences.en: "We aim to set the standard for open source innovation.
sentences.zh: “我们的目标是为开源创新设定标准。

sentences.en: A truly open Fedora Project is what makes that possible."
sentences.zh: 真正开放的 Fedora 项目使这一切成为可能。”

blocks.html_en: Sandbox

sentences.en: Sandbox
sentences.zh: 沙盒

blocks.html_en: In the past year or so, though, the project's processes that have worked until now seem to be coming into question more and more often: usually when they seem to butt up against Red Hat's priorities. For example, last year's deliberation on Fedora's AI-assisted contribution policy that showed a disconnect between Red Hat's desire to experiment with AI in Fedora and contributors who wanted Fedora's policy to be much less friendly to AI.

sentences.en: In the past year or so, though, the project's processes that have worked until now seem to be coming into question more and more often: usually when they seem to butt up against Red Hat's priorities.
sentences.zh: 然而，在过去一年左右的时间里，迄今为止一直有效的项目流程似乎越来越频繁地受到质疑：通常是当它们似乎与红帽的优先事项相抵触时。

sentences.en: For example, last year's deliberation on Fedora's AI-assisted contribution policy that showed a disconnect between Red Hat's desire to experiment with AI in Fedora and contributors who wanted Fedora's policy to be much less friendly to AI.
sentences.zh: 例如，去年对 Fedora 的人工智能辅助贡献政策的审议表明，红帽希望在 Fedora 中试验人工智能，而贡献者则希望 Fedora 的政策对人工智能不太友好。

blocks.html_en: In March, current FPL Jef Spaleta proposed a technology-innovation-lifecycle process he dubbed the "Fedora Sandbox" for "experimental features, components, output, process, or services".

sentences.en: In March, current FPL Jef Spaleta proposed a technology-innovation-lifecycle process he dubbed the "Fedora Sandbox" for "experimental features, components, output, process, or services".
sentences.zh: 今年 3 月，现任 FPL Jef Spaleta 提出了一个技术创新生命周期流程，他将其称为“Fedora 沙箱”，用于“实验功能、组件、输出、流程或服务”。

blocks.html_en: The driver for Spaleta's sandbox idea seemed to be related to frustrations that Red Hat leadership had with pushing its experiments into Fedora; the project's existing processes, which involve compliance with Fedora's strict packaging and license policies as well as a need to gain community buy-in, could slow or stymie acceptance of things Red Hat was going to do for RHEL anyway.

sentences.en: The driver for Spaleta's sandbox idea seemed to be related to frustrations that Red Hat leadership had with pushing its experiments into Fedora; the project's existing processes, which involve compliance with Fedora's strict packaging and license policies as well as a need to gain community buy-in, could slow or stymie acceptance of things Red Hat was going to do for RHEL anyway.
sentences.zh: Spaleta 沙盒想法的驱动力似乎与红帽领导层在将其实验推向 Fedora 时遇到的挫折有关；该项目的现有流程涉及遵守 Fedora 严格的打包和许可政策以及获得社区支持的需要，这可能会减缓或阻碍人们对红帽为 RHEL 所做的事情的接受。

blocks.html_en: Having to maintain those projects outside of Fedora, which ultimately serves as the foundation for a RHEL release, is a likely source of frustration for folks working on both—not to mention their managers and Red Hat leadership who (not unreasonably) just want features to land in RHEL on schedule to make customers happy.

sentences.en: Having to maintain those projects outside of Fedora, which ultimately serves as the foundation for a RHEL release, is a likely source of frustration for folks working on both—not to mention their managers and Red Hat leadership who (not unreasonably) just want features to land in RHEL on schedule to make customers happy.
sentences.zh: 必须在 Fedora 之外维护这些项目（Fedora 最终将成为 RHEL 版本的基础），这可能是从事这两个项目的人员感到沮丧的原因，更不用说他们的经理和红帽领导层了，他们只是希望功能按计划登陆 RHEL 以使客户满意（这并非没有道理）。

blocks.html_en: Spaleta's sandbox process would have allowed experiments to be conducted in Fedora even if they broke "non legally binding" policies as long as they had a "reasonable path forward towards resolution" before being fully integrated into the project or distribution. He proposed that the sandbox would be in addition to the existing change processes and community initiatives that can be used to set longer-term goals for Fedora, such as the completed initiative to create the Fedora IoT project, or the current Git Forge initiative to replace the Pagure collaboration platform with the Forgejo-based Fedora Forge service. The sandbox proposal has not been accepted (or rejected) yet.

sentences.en: Spaleta's sandbox process would have allowed experiments to be conducted in Fedora even if they broke "non legally binding" policies as long as they had a "reasonable path forward towards resolution" before being fully integrated into the project or distribution.
sentences.zh: Spaleta 的沙箱进程将允许在 Fedora 中进行实验，即使它们违反了“不具有法律约束力”的政策，只要它们在完全集成到项目或发行版之前有“合理的解决路径”。

sentences.en: He proposed that the sandbox would be in addition to the existing change processes and community initiatives that can be used to set longer-term goals for Fedora, such as the completed initiative to create the Fedora IoT project, or the current Git Forge initiative to replace the Pagure collaboration platform with the Forgejo-based Fedora Forge service.
sentences.zh: 他提议，沙盒将是对现有变更流程和社区计划的补充，可用于为 Fedora 设定长期目标，例如创建 Fedora IoT 项目的已完成计划，或当前的 Git Forge 计划，用基于 Forgejo 的 Fedora Forge 服务取代 Pagure 协作平台。

sentences.en: The sandbox proposal has not been accepted (or rejected) yet.
sentences.zh: 沙盒提案尚未被接受（或拒绝）。

blocks.html_en: Initiative friction

sentences.en: Initiative friction
sentences.zh: 主动摩擦

blocks.html_en: Red Hat developer Gordon Messmer proposed an AI developer desktop initiative, on March 31, with an aim to "build a thriving community around AI technologies" within Fedora. That proposal met with some opposition from the community, with objections ranging from a dislike of AI to complaints about potentially changing Fedora's policy for out-of-tree kernel modules. Ultimately the proposal was discussed by the Fedora Council, initially approved, and then blocked at the last minute when council member Justin Wheeler changed his vote on May 8. Council member Miro Hrončok, likewise, also changed his vote on May 13 saying that "the Fedora community is not supportive of this initiative as is".

sentences.en: Red Hat developer Gordon Messmer proposed an AI developer desktop initiative, on March 31, with an aim to "build a thriving community around AI technologies" within Fedora.
sentences.zh: 红帽开发人员 Gordon Messmer 于 3 月 31 日提出了一项人工智能开发桌面计划，旨在在 Fedora 内“围绕人工智能技术建立一个繁荣的社区”。

sentences.en: That proposal met with some opposition from the community, with objections ranging from a dislike of AI to complaints about potentially changing Fedora's policy for out-of-tree kernel modules.
sentences.zh: 该提案遭到了社区的一些反对，反对意见包括对人工智能的厌恶，以及对可能改变 Fedora 树外内核模块政策的抱怨。

sentences.en: Ultimately the proposal was discussed by the Fedora Council, initially approved, and then blocked at the last minute when council member Justin Wheeler changed his vote on May 8.
sentences.zh: 最终，该提案由 Fedora 理事会进行了讨论，最初获得批准，但在最后一刻因理事会成员贾斯汀·惠勒 (Justin Wheeler) 于 5 月 8 日改变投票而被否决。

sentences.en: Council member Miro Hrončok, likewise, also changed his vote on May 13 saying that "the Fedora community is not supportive of this initiative as is".
sentences.zh: 同样，理事会成员 Miro Hrončok 也在 5 月 13 日改变了投票，称“Fedora 社区不支持这一举措”。

blocks.html_en: Another Red Hat idea, an automated approach to building an operating system called Project Hummingbird, was raised on the Fedora development list in April. It was greeted with some interest, as well as some confusion about how it might differ from other Fedora variants, but it didn't seem to face much opposition. It was never formally proposed, though McCarty said he planned to do so through Spaleta's sandbox initiative.

sentences.en: Another Red Hat idea, an automated approach to building an operating system called Project Hummingbird, was raised on the Fedora development list in April.
sentences.zh: 红帽的另一个想法是一种构建名为 Project Hummingbird 的操作系统的自动化方法，于 4 月份在 Fedora 开发列表中提出。

sentences.en: It was greeted with some interest, as well as some confusion about how it might differ from other Fedora variants, but it didn't seem to face much opposition.
sentences.zh: 它引起了一些兴趣，也有人对它与其他 Fedora 变体的不同之处感到困惑，但它似乎没有遇到太多反对。

sentences.en: It was never formally proposed, though McCarty said he planned to do so through Spaleta's sandbox initiative.
sentences.zh: 尽管麦卡蒂表示他计划通过斯帕莱塔的沙盒计划来实现这一目标，但它从未被正式提出。

blocks.html_en: Instead, Red Hat bypassed public processes entirely. It asked the council in private for approval to use the Fedora trademark so that it could announce the project at Red Hat Summit on May 12. Fedora contributors outside of Red Hat were surprised and confused by the announcement. Michael Gruber, for example, wondered whether Hummingbird was legitimately a Fedora project or not. "There might be even some good ideas in there, but given how this started and how it is communicated I can put zero trust in this."

sentences.en: Instead, Red Hat bypassed public processes entirely.
sentences.zh: 相反，红帽完全绕过了公共流程。

sentences.en: It asked the council in private for approval to use the Fedora trademark so that it could announce the project at Red Hat Summit on May 12.
sentences.zh: 它私下请求理事会批准使用 Fedora 商标，以便能够在 5 月 12 日的红帽峰会上宣布该项目。

sentences.en: Fedora contributors outside of Red Hat were surprised and confused by the announcement.
sentences.zh: 红帽以外的 Fedora 贡献者对这一公告感到惊讶和困惑。

sentences.en: Michael Gruber, for example, wondered whether Hummingbird was legitimately a Fedora project or not.
sentences.zh: 例如，Michael Gruber 想知道 Hummingbird 是否是合法的 Fedora 项目。

sentences.en: "There might be even some good ideas in there, but given how this started and how it is communicated I can put zero trust in this."
sentences.zh: “其中甚至可能有一些好主意，但考虑到它是如何开始的以及它是如何传达的，我可以对此采取零信任。”

blocks.html_en: Red Hat employee and Fedora contributor Adam Williamson said it would have been ideal for Red Hat to make the request more openly, but he was happy that the company was trying to do the Hummingbird project within Fedora rather than doing an end run around the project. The more Red Hat has to do outside Fedora, he reasoned, the greater the risk that the company will question its funding of the project.

sentences.en: Red Hat employee and Fedora contributor Adam Williamson said it would have been ideal for Red Hat to make the request more openly, but he was happy that the company was trying to do the Hummingbird project within Fedora rather than doing an end run around the project.
sentences.zh: 红帽员工兼 Fedora 贡献者 Adam Williamson 表示，红帽更公开地提出请求是理想的选择，但他很高兴该公司试图在 Fedora 中开展 Hummingbird 项目，而不是围绕该项目进行最终运行。

sentences.en: The more Red Hat has to do outside Fedora, he reasoned, the greater the risk that the company will question its funding of the project.
sentences.zh: 他推断，红帽在 Fedora 之外要做的事情越多，该公司质疑该项目资金的风险就越大。

blocks.html_en:
From inside Red Hat, especially if you don't work on Fedora, it is possible/tempting to look at Fedora as a source of strife. It's got all these people in it, with opinions, who aren't on the payroll! You can't tell them what to do or think or complain to their manager! They have eternal arguments about everything! You have to write a wiki page and convince some person you've never heard of that your idea is good! Who needs this?

So we're kind of constantly fighting a tendency for RH to just spin stuff up in channels it 100% controls, which tends to seem easiest at first then turn out to be a mess after a few years.

sentences.zh: 从 Red Hat 内部来看，特别是如果您不在 Fedora 上工作，则可能/很容易将 Fedora 视为冲突的根源。

sentences.zh: 里面有很多有意见的人，但他们不在工资单上！

sentences.en: You can't tell them what to do or think or complain to their manager!
sentences.zh: 您不能告诉他们该做什么或想什么或向他们的经理抱怨！

sentences.en: They have eternal arguments about everything!
sentences.zh: 他们对任何事情都有永恒的争论！

sentences.en: You have to write a wiki page and convince some person you've never heard of that your idea is good!
sentences.zh: 您必须编写一个 wiki 页面并让一些您从未听说过的人相信您的想法很好！

sentences.en: Who needs this?
sentences.zh: 谁需要这个？

sentences.en: So we're kind of constantly fighting a tendency for RH to just spin stuff up in channels it 100% controls, which tends to seem easiest at first then turn out to be a mess after a few years.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们一直在与 RH 在它 100% 控制的通道中旋转东西的倾向作斗争，这往往一开始看起来很简单，但几年后就会变得一团糟。

sentences.en: So we're kind of constantly fighting a tendency for RH to just spin stuff up in channels it 100% controls, which tends to seem easiest at first then turn out to be a mess after a few years.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们一直在与 RH 在它 100% 控制的通道中旋转东西的倾向作斗争，这往往一开始看起来很简单，但几年后就会变得一团糟。

blocks.html_en: Christopher Klooz, however, worried that the funding justification meant that Fedora could be "step by step transformed into a corporate unit of RH". He added that it was already unclear "when the Council acts as agent of RH and when as agent of the community".

sentences.en: Christopher Klooz, however, worried that the funding justification meant that Fedora could be "step by step transformed into a corporate unit of RH".
sentences.zh: 然而，Christopher Klooz 担心，融资理由意味着 Fedora 可能“逐步转变为 RH 的一个企业部门”。

sentences.en: He added that it was already unclear "when the Council acts as agent of RH and when as agent of the community".
sentences.zh: 他补充说，目前尚不清楚“理事会何时充当 RH 的代理人，何时充当社区的代理人”。

blocks.html_en: Williamson replied that he understood the point, but said that this was a "an ever-present tension" that Fedora has had to manage nearly as long as it's been around:

sentences.en: Williamson replied that he understood the point, but said that this was a "an ever-present tension" that Fedora has had to manage nearly as long as it's been around:
sentences.zh: Williamson 回答说他理解这一点，但表示这是一种“始终存在的紧张局势”，Fedora 几乎自存在以来就必须应对：

blocks.html_en: The way Fedora can "compete" is not by turning into the thing some parts of RH might superficially think they want - an upstream project that's open source in license terms but closely-controlled in governance - but by being the thing they need - a loosely-coupled upstream project with passionate and involved people who will make things awkward and uncomfortable at times but usually produce a better end result over the long term, and act as a useful check on RH's perceptions at times.

sentences.en: The way Fedora can "compete" is not by turning into the thing some parts of RH might superficially think they want - an upstream project that's open source in license terms but closely-controlled in governance - but by being the thing they need - a loosely-coupled upstream project with passionate and involved people who will make things awkward and uncomfortable at times but usually produce a better end result over the long term, and act as a useful check on RH's perceptions at times.
sentences.zh: Fedora 能够“竞争”的方式并不是变成 RH 的某些部分表面上认为他们想要的东西 - 一个在许可条款上开源但在治理方面受到严格控制的上游项目 - 而是成为他们需要的东西 - 一个松散耦合的上游项目，其中有充满热情和参与的人员，这些人有时会让事情变得尴尬和不舒服，但从长远来看通常会产生更好的最终结果，并且有时可以对 RH 的看法进行有用的检查。

blocks.html_en: Red Hat employees who care about Fedora, he said, have to keep selling that vision and making sure that it works. The Hummingbird discussion petered out not long after Williamson's reply, but it appears that Fedora's decision-making bodies have been mulling over how things are done.

sentences.en: Red Hat employees who care about Fedora, he said, have to keep selling that vision and making sure that it works.
sentences.zh: 他说，关心 Fedora 的红帽员工必须继续推销这一愿景并确保其发挥作用。

sentences.en: The Hummingbird discussion petered out not long after Williamson's reply, but it appears that Fedora's decision-making bodies have been mulling over how things are done.
sentences.zh: 在 Williamson 回复后不久，关于蜂鸟的讨论就逐渐平息，但 Fedora 的决策机构似乎一直在考虑如何完成事情。

blocks.html_en: Hit pause

sentences.en: Hit pause
sentences.zh: 点击暂停

blocks.html_en: On July 1, Aoife Moloney, Fedora's "change wrangler", announced that the Fedora Council was proposing a pause to Fedora's community initiatives process. Existing initiatives would continue as planned, she said, though "the administrative framework around them may evolve".

sentences.en: On July 1, Aoife Moloney, Fedora's "change wrangler", announced that the Fedora Council was proposing a pause to Fedora's community initiatives process.
sentences.zh: 7 月 1 日，Fedora 的“变革争论者”Aoife Moloney 宣布 Fedora 委员会建议暂停 Fedora 的社区倡议流程。

sentences.en: Existing initiatives would continue as planned, she said, though "the administrative framework around them may evolve".
sentences.zh: 她说，现有举措将按计划继续进行，尽管“围绕这些举措的管理框架可能会发生变化”。

blocks.html_en: The AI developer desktop, she said, had shown that the initiatives process had failed as a framework "where new ideas can surface, receive respectful feedback, and gain Council support for work that fits the project's present and/or future". The nature of the failure, however, was unspecified. One can imagine that the various participants in that discussion might well agree that something had not gone well, but what that something was would depend entirely on the observer's point of view.

sentences.en: The AI developer desktop, she said, had shown that the initiatives process had failed as a framework "where new ideas can surface, receive respectful feedback, and gain Council support for work that fits the project's present and/or future".
sentences.zh: 她说，人工智能开发人员桌面已经表明，该倡议流程作为一个框架是失败的，“新想法可以浮出水面，得到尊重的反馈，并获得理事会对适合项目现在和/或未来的工作的支持”。

sentences.en: The nature of the failure, however, was unspecified.
sentences.zh: 然而，失败的性质并未具体说明。

sentences.en: One can imagine that the various participants in that discussion might well agree that something had not gone well, but what that something was would depend entirely on the observer's point of view.
sentences.zh: 人们可以想象，讨论中的各个参与者很可能都同意某件事进展不顺利，但那件事是什么将完全取决于观察者的观点。

blocks.html_en: The council, Moloney said, wanted to work out a new method of setting strategic direction "in an open, transparent way that more intentionally includes the community voice." The council recognized that it needed to be "better at being more open in our discussions and decision making" because much of the work that leads to proposals "happens under the radar before official approval processes kick in". At the moment the existing "approval pipeline" for initiatives involves the council performing trademark review, and then FESCo reviewing change proposals. That works well, she said, but misses "early and inclusive discussion for everyone across the project". Therefore, the council would be looking at the sandbox proposal closely as an alternative to initiatives or as a complement to some other process that it might develop.

sentences.en: The council, Moloney said, wanted to work out a new method of setting strategic direction "in an open, transparent way that more intentionally includes the community voice."
sentences.zh: 莫洛尼表示，该委员会希望制定一种“以开放、透明的方式，更有意地纳入社区声音”来制定战略方向的新方法。

sentences.en: The council recognized that it needed to be "better at being more open in our discussions and decision making" because much of the work that leads to proposals "happens under the radar before official approval processes kick in".
sentences.zh: 该委员会认识到，它需要“更好地在讨论和决策中更加开放”，因为导致提案的大部分工作“都是在官方批准程序启动之前进行的”。

sentences.en: At the moment the existing "approval pipeline" for initiatives involves the council performing trademark review, and then FESCo reviewing change proposals.
sentences.zh: 目前，现有的举措“审批流程”包括理事会进行商标审查，然后 FESCo 审查变更提案。

sentences.en: That works well, she said, but misses "early and inclusive discussion for everyone across the project".
sentences.zh: 她说，这种做法效果很好，但错过了“整个项目中每个人的早期和包容性讨论”。

sentences.en: Therefore, the council would be looking at the sandbox proposal closely as an alternative to initiatives or as a complement to some other process that it might develop.
sentences.zh: 因此，理事会将密切关注沙盒提案，将其作为倡议的替代方案或作为其可能开发的其他流程的补充。

blocks.html_en: The announcement phrased the pause as a proposal, but Moloney also closed the discussion on the AI developer desktop proposal saying that the council was now unable to consider it "as a result of halting the Community Initiatives process." The council would return to the question of "whether Community initiatives should be retired or revamped once this discussion has reached some kind of conclusion".

sentences.en: The announcement phrased the pause as a proposal, but Moloney also closed the discussion on the AI developer desktop proposal saying that the council was now unable to consider it "as a result of halting the Community Initiatives process."
sentences.zh: 该公告将暂停表述为一项提案，但莫洛尼也结束了对人工智能开发者桌面提案的讨论，称“由于社区倡议流程停止”，理事会现在无法考虑该提案。

sentences.en: The council would return to the question of "whether Community initiatives should be retired or revamped once this discussion has reached some kind of conclusion".
sentences.zh: 理事会将回到“一旦讨论得出某种结论，社区倡议是否应该撤销或修改”的问题。

blocks.html_en: Changes to changes

sentences.en: Changes to changes
sentences.zh: 改变的改变

blocks.html_en: Shortly before the council had made its announcement, FESCo member "Maxwell G" started a discussion on the Fedora development mailing list to gather feedback on how the changes process could be improved. He said he was not proposing anything specific, but wanted to throw out some ideas and see what other people thought.

sentences.en: Shortly before the council had made its announcement, FESCo member "Maxwell G" started a discussion on the Fedora development mailing list to gather feedback on how the changes process could be improved.
sentences.zh: 在理事会发布公告之前不久，FESCo 成员“Maxwell G”开始在 Fedora 开发邮件列表上进行讨论，以收集有关如何改进变更流程的反馈。

sentences.en: He said he was not proposing anything specific, but wanted to throw out some ideas and see what other people thought.
sentences.zh: 他说他并不是提出任何具体的建议，只是想提出一些想法，看看其他人的想法。

blocks.html_en: For example, he wondered if it was time to move away from using Fedora's wiki for proposals, as well as the Wikitext formatting that goes along with it. The formatting often escapes the wiki and makes its way to other fora; for example, the Btrfs change proposal for Fedora 33 from 2020 has a combination of plain text, MediaWiki formatting, and HTML <span> tags that makes it unpleasant to attempt to decipher in any mail client.

sentences.en: For example, he wondered if it was time to move away from using Fedora's wiki for proposals, as well as the Wikitext formatting that goes along with it.
sentences.zh: 例如，他想知道是否是时候放弃使用 Fedora 的 wiki 来提出建议，以及随之而来的 Wikitext 格式。

sentences.en: The formatting often escapes the wiki and makes its way to other fora; for example, the Btrfs change proposal for Fedora 33 from 2020 has a combination of plain text, MediaWiki formatting, and HTML <span> tags that makes it unpleasant to attempt to decipher in any mail client.
sentences.zh: 这种格式通常会从 wiki 中逃脱，并进入其他论坛；例如，从 2020 年起针对 Fedora 33 的 Btrfs 变更提案结合了纯文本、MediaWiki 格式和 HTML <span> 标记，这使得在任何邮件客户端中尝试解密都变得不那么愉快。

blocks.html_en: He suggested that changes should be in Markdown or "some format that can easily be converted", and stored as text files in a Git repository rather than on the wiki. The change owners could file proposals as a merge request, which would be reviewed and merged by the change wrangler. He also floated the idea of moving away from the Discourse forum as the primary source of truth for change proposals; he also wanted to stop the practice of having discussions on changes take place both on the forum and Fedora's development list:

sentences.en: He suggested that changes should be in Markdown or "some format that can easily be converted", and stored as text files in a Git repository rather than on the wiki.
sentences.zh: 他建议更改应该采用 Markdown 或“某种可以轻松转换的格式”，并作为文本文件存储在 Git 存储库中，而不是存储在 wiki 中。

sentences.en: The change owners could file proposals as a merge request, which would be reviewed and merged by the change wrangler.
sentences.zh: 变更所有者可以将提案作为合并请求提交，变更管理员将对其进行审查和合并。

sentences.en: He also floated the idea of moving away from the Discourse forum as the primary source of truth for change proposals; he also wanted to stop the practice of having discussions on changes take place both on the forum and Fedora's development list:
sentences.zh: 他还提出了放弃将 Discourse 论坛作为变革提案的主要事实来源的想法；他还希望停止在论坛和 Fedora 开发列表上讨论变更的做法：

blocks.html_en: Cross-posting to Discourse was proposed as an experiment in fesco [ticket] #2989, but there was never a decision made about whether to stop or continue with the experiment. I find the fractured discussions between devel@ and Discourse hard to follow. I think the Discourse setup makes it easier for discussions to "accelerate" or become toxic or repetitive. I don't think it's any better at handling large threads.

sentences.en: Cross-posting to Discourse was proposed as an experiment in fesco [ticket] #2989, but there was never a decision made about whether to stop or continue with the experiment.
sentences.zh: fesco [ticket] #2989 中提议将交叉发布到 Discourse 作为一项实验，但从未决定是否停止或继续该实验。

sentences.en: I find the fractured discussions between devel@ and Discourse hard to follow.
sentences.zh: 我发现 devel@ 和 Discourse 之间支离破碎的讨论很难理解。

sentences.en: I think the Discourse setup makes it easier for discussions to "accelerate" or become toxic or repetitive.
sentences.zh: 我认为话语设置使讨论更容易“加速”或变得有毒或重复。

sentences.en: I don't think it's any better at handling large threads.
sentences.zh: 我认为它在处理大线程方面并没有更好。

blocks.html_en: The suggestion to consolidate discussions on the mailing list seemed popular. Michal Schorm agreed that splitting discussions between the mailing list and forum was bad, especially for the change owners who had to follow both. Björn Persson also agreed that the discussions needed to be consolidated, but he observed that it was much worse than just following a forum and mailing list discussion. "When I went through the Change process, the state of the Change was fractured over the wiki, Pagure, Bugzilla and even Gitlab." He counted ten different discussion fora and issue trackers, all in their own silos. "At least Pagure and Bugzilla are good at sending me email when someone writes a comment."

sentences.en: The suggestion to consolidate discussions on the mailing list seemed popular.
sentences.zh: 合并邮件列表上的讨论的建议似乎很受欢迎。

sentences.en: Michal Schorm agreed that splitting discussions between the mailing list and forum was bad, especially for the change owners who had to follow both.
sentences.zh: Michal Schorm 同意在邮件列表和论坛之间划分讨论是不好的，特别是对于必须同时关注两者的变更所有者而言。

sentences.en: Björn Persson also agreed that the discussions needed to be consolidated, but he observed that it was much worse than just following a forum and mailing list discussion.
sentences.zh: Björn Persson 也同意需要巩固讨论，但他指出这比仅仅遵循论坛和邮件列表讨论要糟糕得多。

sentences.en: "When I went through the Change process, the state of the Change was fractured over the wiki, Pagure, Bugzilla and even Gitlab."
sentences.zh: “当我经历变更流程时，变更的状态在 wiki、Pagure、Bugzilla 甚至 Gitlab 上都是支离破碎的。”

sentences.en: He counted ten different discussion fora and issue trackers, all in their own silos.
sentences.zh: 他数了数十个不同的讨论论坛和问题跟踪器，它们都在各自的孤岛中。

sentences.en: "At least Pagure and Bugzilla are good at sending me email when someone writes a comment."
sentences.zh: “至少 Pagure 和 Bugzilla 擅长在有人发表评论时向我发送电子邮件。”

blocks.html_en: Kevin Fenzi, however, thought that the practice of using the forum for change requests should continue. He acknowledged that there were some problems with that, but it meant Fedora received feedback "from people who are not otherwise involved and sometimes [it is] very useful". He felt that it was important to hear from new voices and to accept feedback "in the place where they are".

sentences.en: Kevin Fenzi, however, thought that the practice of using the forum for change requests should continue.
sentences.zh: 然而，Kevin Fenzi 认为使用论坛提出变更请求的做法应该继续下去。

sentences.en: He acknowledged that there were some problems with that, but it meant Fedora received feedback "from people who are not otherwise involved and sometimes [it is] very useful".
sentences.zh: 他承认这存在一些问题，但这意味着 Fedora 收到了“来自没有其他参与的人的反馈，有时[它]非常有用”。

sentences.en: He felt that it was important to hear from new voices and to accept feedback "in the place where they are".
sentences.zh: 他认为听取新的声音并“在他们所在的地方”接受反馈非常重要。

blocks.html_en: Former Fedora program manager Ben Cotton, who had worked closely with the change process during his tenure, thought that the process should be modernized, but suggested that a better approach would be to think about what the process should look like at a high level, then think about how to implement it.

sentences.en: Former Fedora program manager Ben Cotton, who had worked closely with the change process during his tenure, thought that the process should be modernized, but suggested that a better approach would be to think about what the process should look like at a high level, then think about how to implement it.
sentences.zh: 前 Fedora 项目经理 Ben Cotton 在任职期间与变更流程密切合作，他认为流程应该现代化，但建议更好的方法是从高层次考虑流程应该是什么样子，然后考虑如何实施它。

blocks.html_en: There was a suggestion from Martin Kolman that Fedora should build a simple web application to manage the change process. It would be less daunting for new contributors, he said, and could be designed to validate information before a change was submitted. The idea of another homegrown application to maintain, however, did not win supporters. Daniel P. Berrangé said that Fedora had "a long (and disappointing) track record" of building things and then being unable to support them in the long run. "I can't see it being a good use of resources to build & maintain a custom app for this."

sentences.en: There was a suggestion from Martin Kolman that Fedora should build a simple web application to manage the change process.
sentences.zh: Martin Kolman 建议 Fedora 应该构建一个简单的 Web 应用程序来管理变更过程。

sentences.en: It would be less daunting for new contributors, he said, and could be designed to validate information before a change was submitted.
sentences.zh: 他说，这对于新贡献者来说不会那么令人畏惧，并且可以设计为在提交更改之前验证信息。

sentences.en: The idea of another homegrown application to maintain, however, did not win supporters.
sentences.zh: 然而，维护另一个本土应用程序的想法并没有赢得支持者。

sentences.en: Daniel P.
sentences.zh: 丹尼尔·P。

sentences.en: Berrangé said that Fedora had "a long (and disappointing) track record" of building things and then being unable to support them in the long run.
sentences.zh: Berrangé 表示，Fedora 在构建事物方面有着“长期（且令人失望的）记录”，但从长远来看却无法支持它们。

sentences.en: "I can't see it being a good use of resources to build & maintain a custom app for this."
sentences.zh: “我不认为为此构建和维护自定义应用程序是一种很好的资源利用方式。”

blocks.html_en: Moloney, who works with changes as part of her job, weighed in on June 30. She said she had agreed with some of the suggestions, such as restricting change discussions to the mailing list, but asked that participants in the discussion to ask themselves how much of hands-on involvement they had with the mechanics of the process "before suggesting ways to engineer a brand new way to do it". She also noted that there were many moving parts already, such as the move to Fedora Forge, and thought it might be a good idea to let the dust settle from those "and then see what we're missing".

sentences.en: Moloney, who works with changes as part of her job, weighed in on June 30.
sentences.zh: 莫洛尼 (Moloney) 的工作一部分就是应对变革，她于 6 月 30 日发表了看法。

sentences.en: She said she had agreed with some of the suggestions, such as restricting change discussions to the mailing list, but asked that participants in the discussion to ask themselves how much of hands-on involvement they had with the mechanics of the process "before suggesting ways to engineer a brand new way to do it".
sentences.zh: 她说她同意其中的一些建议，例如将变更讨论限制在邮件列表中，但要求讨论的参与者“在提出设计全新方法之前”先问问自己，他们对流程机制有多少实际参与。

sentences.en: She also noted that there were many moving parts already, such as the move to Fedora Forge, and thought it might be a good idea to let the dust settle from those "and then see what we're missing".
sentences.zh: 她还指出，已经有许多移动部分，例如迁移到 Fedora Forge，并认为让这些尘埃落定“然后看看我们缺少什么”可能是个好主意。

blocks.html_en: Red Hat engineering manager Brendan Conoboy also spoke up with his view of Fedora's processes from the outside looking in. Overall, he thought that the change processes work pretty well for the Linux distribution. "Here's what doesn't work: changes that fit poorly inside existing processes." He cited the recent two-factor authentication discussion and the AI developer desktop proposal as examples. "The bar to clear to initiate change in a way every interest feels respected is tough to such a degree that even initiating a conversation is fraught."

sentences.en: Red Hat engineering manager Brendan Conoboy also spoke up with his view of Fedora's processes from the outside looking in.
sentences.zh: Red Hat 工程经理 Brendan Conoboy 也表达了他从外部到内部对 Fedora 流程的看法。

sentences.en: Overall, he thought that the change processes work pretty well for the Linux distribution.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，他认为变更过程对于 Linux 发行版来说效果很好。

sentences.en: "Here's what doesn't work: changes that fit poorly inside existing processes."
sentences.zh: “这是行不通的：与现有流程不相适应的变更。”

sentences.en: He cited the recent two-factor authentication discussion and the AI developer desktop proposal as examples.
sentences.zh: 他引用了最近的双因素身份验证讨论和人工智能开发者桌面提案作为例子。

sentences.en: "The bar to clear to initiate change in a way every interest feels respected is tough to such a degree that even initiating a conversation is fraught."
sentences.zh: “以让每个利益相关者都感到受到尊重的方式发起变革的门槛非常高，以至于即使发起对话也会充满挑战。”

blocks.html_en: Maxwell G pointed out that the AI discussion "hit a lot of pain points", some of which were procedural (such as the council failing to clearly announce its intent to vote on the proposal), but there were also social and political disagreements as well. In any case, he said, that was an initiative rather than a change proposal—and that process was under separate review by the council. Conoboy replied, "we knew the [AI] subject matter was controversial, and it seemed that following a documented process would provide a better framework for constructive feedback". Perhaps it did, he said, but it might have gone better if the idea had been socialized with FESCo first.

sentences.en: Maxwell G pointed out that the AI discussion "hit a lot of pain points", some of which were procedural (such as the council failing to clearly announce its intent to vote on the proposal), but there were also social and political disagreements as well.
sentences.zh: Maxwell G 指出，人工智能讨论“触及了很多痛点”，其中一些是程序性的（例如理事会未能明确宣布其对该提案进行投票的意图），但也存在社会和政治分歧。

sentences.en: In any case, he said, that was an initiative rather than a change proposal—and that process was under separate review by the council.
sentences.zh: 他说，无论如何，这是一项倡议，而不是一项变革提案，而且该过程正在接受理事会的单独审查。

sentences.en: Conoboy replied, "we knew the [AI] subject matter was controversial, and it seemed that following a documented process would provide a better framework for constructive feedback".
sentences.zh: Conoboy 回答说：“我们知道 [AI] 主题存在争议，而且遵循记录的流程似乎将为建设性反馈提供更好的框架”。

sentences.en: Perhaps it did, he said, but it might have gone better if the idea had been socialized with FESCo first.
sentences.zh: 他说，也许确实如此，但如果这个想法首先与 FESCo 进行交流，情况可能会更好。

blocks.html_en: Outcome

sentences.en: Outcome
sentences.zh: 结果

blocks.html_en: After the discussion had wound down Maxwell G replied on July 7, with his summary of the conversation. His first takeaway was that he should have had a more detailed problem statement that had more context about what he was looking for from the discussion. He found that there was some support for a Git-based workflow, but the wiki-based workflow also had many supporters; many ideas were put forth, but no clear winners.

sentences.en: After the discussion had wound down Maxwell G replied on July 7, with his summary of the conversation.
sentences.zh: 讨论结束后，Maxwell G 于 7 月 7 日做出了回复，并附上了他对谈话的总结。

sentences.en: His first takeaway was that he should have had a more detailed problem statement that had more context about what he was looking for from the discussion.
sentences.zh: 他的第一个收获是，他应该有一个更详细的问题陈述，其中包含更多有关他从讨论中寻找的内容的背景信息。

sentences.en: He found that there was some support for a Git-based workflow, but the wiki-based workflow also had many supporters; many ideas were put forth, but no clear winners.
sentences.zh: 他发现基于 Git 的工作流程有一些支持，但基于 wiki 的工作流程也有很多支持者；人们提出了许多想法，但没有明显的赢家。

blocks.html_en: What was clear, however, was that the split between the mailing list and forum "creates a frustrating burden for Change Owners and other people trying to follow the discussion". The project had tried the "split-brain approach for development discussion" for three years, and it was not working. He said he would focus on solving that pain point first, and then would come back with ideas for a different wiki-based process at a later date.

sentences.en: What was clear, however, was that the split between the mailing list and forum "creates a frustrating burden for Change Owners and other people trying to follow the discussion".
sentences.zh: 然而，显而易见的是，邮件列表和论坛之间的分离“给变更所有者和其他试图关注讨论的人带来了令人沮丧的负担”。

sentences.en: The project had tried the "split-brain approach for development discussion" for three years, and it was not working.
sentences.zh: 该项目尝试了三年的“裂脑开发讨论方式”，但没有奏效。

sentences.en: He said he would focus on solving that pain point first, and then would come back with ideas for a different wiki-based process at a later date.
sentences.zh: 他表示，他将首先专注于解决这个痛点，然后稍后再提出基于维基的不同流程的想法。

blocks.html_en: Given that it is vacation season in much of the world, there is a good chance that the various efforts to modify Fedora's processes will see little movement in the next month or two. It seems likely, though, that there will be some shakeups to the project's processes once Fedora contributors return from holiday and pick up where these conversations have left off.

sentences.en: Given that it is vacation season in much of the world, there is a good chance that the various efforts to modify Fedora's processes will see little movement in the next month or two.
sentences.zh: 鉴于世界大部分地区正值假期，修改 Fedora 流程的各种努力很可能在未来一两个月内不会有什么进展。

sentences.en: It seems likely, though, that there will be some shakeups to the project's processes once Fedora contributors return from holiday and pick up where these conversations have left off.
sentences.zh: 不过，一旦 Fedora 贡献者度假归来并继续这些对话，该项目的流程似乎可能会发生一些变化。

content_html_zh:

Activate your trial subscription now and see why thousands of readers subscribe to LWN.net.

Fedora 项目因拥有一套适用于几乎所有事物的明确定义的流程而闻名。 它具有广泛的打包指南，可处理创建 RPM 来安装软件的复杂性，以及管理与运输软件相关的法律问题的流程。 Fedora 还有一个定义明确的变更流程，用于处理独立的技术变更以及发行版的重大变更以及出现的其他问题。 但目前，该项目似乎正在经历某种中年危机，因为它同时重新检查了几个变革流程，以确定它们是否仍然有效。

进化

作为一个由厂商赞助的项目，Fedora 在服务用户、贡献者社区和企业大师方面始终要走一条艰难的道路；让一方最快乐的事情可能会成为另一方焦虑的根源。 红帽想要在 Fedora 中试用技术的愿望并不总能让贡献者和用户感到高兴。 志愿者贡献者可能想要推动红帽不感兴趣的想法（至少在当时），甚至可能与其选择相冲突。 例如，Fedora 选择 Btrfs 作为默认文件系统，而 Red Hat 决定在 Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) 中不支持它。

用户希望轻松访问有问题的软件，例如受专利保护的游戏或编解码器，但运送这些软件可能会给红帽带来昂贵的法律麻烦。 尝试使 Fedora 更加用户友好，例如将 $EDITOR 环境变量的默认值设置为 GNU nano，可能不会令某些开发人员满意。

当 Fedora 项目于 2003 年作为 Red Hat Linux 的替代品启动时，它几乎没有任何治理或政策。 早期，红帽曾考虑过创建一个“Fedora 基金会”来赋予该项目一定的独立性，但随后在 2006 年撤回了这一想法。 随着时间的推移，一些在基金会成立时建立的机制，例如 Fedora 董事会和 Fedora Extras 指导委员会，逐渐演变为 Fedora 理事会和 Fedora 工程指导委员会 (FESCo)。

企业和社区的优先事项之间曾多次发生过摩擦；但这些年来该项目或多或少已经发挥了作用。 它通过讨论问题、找到某种共识、记录一路上敲定的政策，然后将其用于新的决策来做到这一点。 例如，请参阅 Tom Callaway 对 Fedora 法律政策如何制定的概述。 多年来，Fedora 的治理和政策已经成为其他开源项目的典范。

该项目的治理有一个不变的事实：红帽拥有最终决定权。 正如前 Fedora 项目负责人 (FPL) Max Spevack 在红帽放弃独立基金会的想法时指出的那样：

红帽“必须”对 Fedora 决策保持一定程度的控制，因为红帽的商业模式“依赖于”Fedora。 红帽为 Fedora 的成功贡献了数百万美元的员工和资源，红帽也承担了 Fedora 的所有法律风险。 因此，Red Hat 有时需要对 Fedora 做出艰难的决定。 我们不会经常这样做，当我们这样做时，我们将尽可能公开地讨论此类决定背后的理由。

他说，仅仅因为红帽拥有对 Fedora 的控制权，并不意味着该公司想要使用它。 它也不想做出有关 Fedora 的所有重要决策：有效的社区驱动决策将是 Fedora 成功的直接衡量标准。 “我们的目标是为开源创新设定标准。 真正开放的 Fedora 项目使这一切成为可能。”

沙盒

然而，在过去一年左右的时间里，迄今为止一直有效的项目流程似乎越来越频繁地受到质疑：通常是当它们似乎与红帽的优先事项相抵触时。 例如，去年对 Fedora 的人工智能辅助贡献政策的审议表明，红帽希望在 Fedora 中试验人工智能，而贡献者则希望 Fedora 的政策对人工智能不太友好。

今年 3 月，现任 FPL Jef Spaleta 提出了一个技术创新生命周期流程，他将其称为“Fedora 沙箱”，用于“实验功能、组件、输出、流程或服务”。

Spaleta 沙盒想法的驱动力似乎与红帽领导层在将其实验推向 Fedora 时遇到的挫折有关；该项目的现有流程涉及遵守 Fedora 严格的打包和许可政策以及获得社区支持的需要，这可能会减缓或阻碍人们对红帽为 RHEL 所做的事情的接受。

必须在 Fedora 之外维护这些项目（Fedora 最终将成为 RHEL 版本的基础），这可能是从事这两个项目的人员感到沮丧的原因，更不用说他们的经理和红帽领导层了，他们只是希望功能按计划登陆 RHEL 以使客户满意（这并非没有道理）。

Spaleta 的沙箱进程将允许在 Fedora 中进行实验，即使它们违反了“不具有法律约束力”的政策，只要它们在完全集成到项目或发行版之前有“合理的解决路径”。 他提议，沙盒将是对现有变更流程和社区计划的补充，可用于为 Fedora 设定长期目标，例如创建 Fedora IoT 项目的已完成计划，或当前的 Git Forge 计划，用基于 Forgejo 的 Fedora Forge 服务取代 Pagure 协作平台。 沙盒提案尚未被接受（或拒绝）。

主动摩擦

红帽开发人员 Gordon Messmer 于 3 月 31 日提出了一项人工智能开发桌面计划，旨在在 Fedora 内“围绕人工智能技术建立一个繁荣的社区”。 该提案遭到了社区的一些反对，反对意见包括对人工智能的厌恶，以及对可能改变 Fedora 树外内核模块政策的抱怨。 最终，该提案由 Fedora 理事会进行了讨论，最初获得批准，但在最后一刻因理事会成员贾斯汀·惠勒 (Justin Wheeler) 于 5 月 8 日改变投票而被否决。 同样，理事会成员 Miro Hrončok 也在 5 月 13 日改变了投票，称“Fedora 社区不支持这一举措”。

红帽的另一个想法是一种构建名为 Project Hummingbird 的操作系统的自动化方法，于 4 月份在 Fedora 开发列表中提出。 它引起了一些兴趣，也有人对它与其他 Fedora 变体的不同之处感到困惑，但它似乎没有遇到太多反对。 尽管麦卡蒂表示他计划通过斯帕莱塔的沙盒计划来实现这一目标，但它从未被正式提出。

相反，红帽完全绕过了公共流程。 它私下请求理事会批准使用 Fedora 商标，以便能够在 5 月 12 日的红帽峰会上宣布该项目。 红帽以外的 Fedora 贡献者对这一公告感到惊讶和困惑。 例如，Michael Gruber 想知道 Hummingbird 是否是合法的 Fedora 项目。 “其中甚至可能有一些好主意，但考虑到它是如何开始的以及它是如何传达的，我可以对此采取零信任。”

红帽员工兼 Fedora 贡献者 Adam Williamson 表示，红帽更公开地提出请求是理想的选择，但他很高兴该公司试图在 Fedora 中开展 Hummingbird 项目，而不是围绕该项目进行最终运行。 他推断，红帽在 Fedora 之外要做的事情越多，该公司质疑该项目资金的风险就越大。

从 Red Hat 内部来看，特别是如果您不在 Fedora 上工作，则可能/很容易将 Fedora 视为冲突的根源。 里面有很多有意见的人，但他们不在工资单上！ 您不能告诉他们该做什么或想什么或向他们的经理抱怨！ 他们对任何事情都有永恒的争论！ 您必须编写一个 wiki 页面并让一些您从未听说过的人相信您的想法很好！ 谁需要这个？ 因此，我们一直在与 RH 在它 100% 控制的通道中旋转东西的倾向作斗争，这往往一开始看起来很简单，但几年后就会变得一团糟。

然而，Christopher Klooz 担心，融资理由意味着 Fedora 可能“逐步转变为 RH 的一个企业部门”。 他补充说，目前尚不清楚“理事会何时充当 RH 的代理人，何时充当社区的代理人”。

Williamson 回答说他理解这一点，但表示这是一种“始终存在的紧张局势”，Fedora 几乎自存在以来就必须应对：

Fedora 能够“竞争”的方式并不是变成 RH 的某些部分表面上认为他们想要的东西 - 一个在许可条款上开源但在治理方面受到严格控制的上游项目 - 而是成为他们需要的东西 - 一个松散耦合的上游项目，其中有充满热情和参与的人员，这些人有时会让事情变得尴尬和不舒服，但从长远来看通常会产生更好的最终结果，并且有时可以对 RH 的看法进行有用的检查。

他说，关心 Fedora 的红帽员工必须继续推销这一愿景并确保其发挥作用。 在 Williamson 回复后不久，关于蜂鸟的讨论就逐渐平息，但 Fedora 的决策机构似乎一直在考虑如何完成事情。

点击暂停

7 月 1 日，Fedora 的“变革争论者”Aoife Moloney 宣布 Fedora 委员会建议暂停 Fedora 的社区倡议流程。 她说，现有举措将按计划继续进行，尽管“围绕这些举措的管理框架可能会发生变化”。

她说，人工智能开发人员桌面已经表明，该倡议流程作为一个框架是失败的，“新想法可以浮出水面，得到尊重的反馈，并获得理事会对适合项目现在和/或未来的工作的支持”。 然而，失败的性质并未具体说明。 人们可以想象，讨论中的各个参与者很可能都同意某件事进展不顺利，但那件事是什么将完全取决于观察者的观点。

莫洛尼表示，该委员会希望制定一种“以开放、透明的方式，更有意地纳入社区声音”来制定战略方向的新方法。 该委员会认识到，它需要“更好地在讨论和决策中更加开放”，因为导致提案的大部分工作“都是在官方批准程序启动之前进行的”。 目前，现有的举措“审批流程”包括理事会进行商标审查，然后 FESCo 审查变更提案。 她说，这种做法效果很好，但错过了“整个项目中每个人的早期和包容性讨论”。 因此，理事会将密切关注沙盒提案，将其作为倡议的替代方案或作为其可能开发的其他流程的补充。

该公告将暂停表述为一项提案，但莫洛尼也结束了对人工智能开发者桌面提案的讨论，称“由于社区倡议流程停止”，理事会现在无法考虑该提案。 理事会将回到“一旦讨论得出某种结论，社区倡议是否应该撤销或修改”的问题。

改变的改变

在理事会发布公告之前不久，FESCo 成员“Maxwell G”开始在 Fedora 开发邮件列表上进行讨论，以收集有关如何改进变更流程的反馈。 他说他并不是提出任何具体的建议，只是想提出一些想法，看看其他人的想法。

例如，他想知道是否是时候放弃使用 Fedora 的 wiki 来提出建议，以及随之而来的 Wikitext 格式。 这种格式通常会从 wiki 中逃脱，并进入其他论坛；例如，从 2020 年起针对 Fedora 33 的 Btrfs 变更提案结合了纯文本、MediaWiki 格式和 HTML <span> 标记，这使得在任何邮件客户端中尝试解密都变得不那么愉快。

他建议更改应该采用 Markdown 或“某种可以轻松转换的格式”，并作为文本文件存储在 Git 存储库中，而不是存储在 wiki 中。 变更所有者可以将提案作为合并请求提交，变更管理员将对其进行审查和合并。 他还提出了放弃将 Discourse 论坛作为变革提案的主要事实来源的想法；他还希望停止在论坛和 Fedora 开发列表上讨论变更的做法：

fesco [ticket] #2989 中提议将交叉发布到 Discourse 作为一项实验，但从未决定是否停止或继续该实验。 我发现 devel@ 和 Discourse 之间支离破碎的讨论很难理解。 我认为话语设置使讨论更容易“加速”或变得有毒或重复。 我认为它在处理大线程方面并没有更好。

合并邮件列表上的讨论的建议似乎很受欢迎。 Michal Schorm 同意在邮件列表和论坛之间划分讨论是不好的，特别是对于必须同时关注两者的变更所有者而言。 Björn Persson 也同意需要巩固讨论，但他指出这比仅仅遵循论坛和邮件列表讨论要糟糕得多。 “当我经历变更流程时，变更的状态在 wiki、Pagure、Bugzilla 甚至 Gitlab 上都是支离破碎的。” 他数了数十个不同的讨论论坛和问题跟踪器，它们都在各自的孤岛中。 “至少 Pagure 和 Bugzilla 擅长在有人发表评论时向我发送电子邮件。”

然而，Kevin Fenzi 认为使用论坛提出变更请求的做法应该继续下去。 他承认这存在一些问题，但这意味着 Fedora 收到了“来自没有其他参与的人的反馈，有时[它]非常有用”。 他认为听取新的声音并“在他们所在的地方”接受反馈非常重要。

前 Fedora 项目经理 Ben Cotton 在任职期间与变更流程密切合作，他认为流程应该现代化，但建议更好的方法是从高层次考虑流程应该是什么样子，然后考虑如何实施它。

Martin Kolman 建议 Fedora 应该构建一个简单的 Web 应用程序来管理变更过程。 他说，这对于新贡献者来说不会那么令人畏惧，并且可以设计为在提交更改之前验证信息。 然而，维护另一个本土应用程序的想法并没有赢得支持者。 丹尼尔·P。 Berrangé 表示，Fedora 在构建事物方面有着“长期（且令人失望的）记录”，但从长远来看却无法支持它们。 “我不认为为此构建和维护自定义应用程序是一种很好的资源利用方式。”

莫洛尼 (Moloney) 的工作一部分就是应对变革，她于 6 月 30 日发表了看法。 她说她同意其中的一些建议，例如将变更讨论限制在邮件列表中，但要求讨论的参与者“在提出设计全新方法之前”先问问自己，他们对流程机制有多少实际参与。 她还指出，已经有许多移动部分，例如迁移到 Fedora Forge，并认为让这些尘埃落定“然后看看我们缺少什么”可能是个好主意。

Red Hat 工程经理 Brendan Conoboy 也表达了他从外部到内部对 Fedora 流程的看法。 总的来说，他认为变更过程对于 Linux 发行版来说效果很好。 “这是行不通的：与现有流程不相适应的变更。” 他引用了最近的双因素身份验证讨论和人工智能开发者桌面提案作为例子。 “以让每个利益相关者都感到受到尊重的方式发起变革的门槛非常高，以至于即使发起对话也会充满挑战。”

Maxwell G 指出，人工智能讨论“触及了很多痛点”，其中一些是程序性的（例如理事会未能明确宣布其对该提案进行投票的意图），但也存在社会和政治分歧。 他说，无论如何，这是一项倡议，而不是一项变革提案，而且该过程正在接受理事会的单独审查。 Conoboy 回答说：“我们知道 [AI] 主题存在争议，而且遵循记录的流程似乎将为建设性反馈提供更好的框架”。 他说，也许确实如此，但如果这个想法首先与 FESCo 进行交流，情况可能会更好。

结果

讨论结束后，Maxwell G 于 7 月 7 日做出了回复，并附上了他对谈话的总结。 他的第一个收获是，他应该有一个更详细的问题陈述，其中包含更多有关他从讨论中寻找的内容的背景信息。 他发现基于 Git 的工作流程有一些支持，但基于 wiki 的工作流程也有很多支持者；人们提出了许多想法，但没有明显的赢家。

然而，显而易见的是，邮件列表和论坛之间的分离“给变更所有者和其他试图关注讨论的人带来了令人沮丧的负担”。 该项目尝试了三年的“裂脑开发讨论方式”，但没有奏效。 他表示，他将首先专注于解决这个痛点，然后稍后再提出基于维基的不同流程的想法。

鉴于世界大部分地区正值假期，修改 Fedora 流程的各种努力很可能在未来一两个月内不会有什么进展。 不过，一旦 Fedora 贡献者度假归来并继续这些对话，该项目的流程似乎可能会发生一些变化。
